aliases: Arlington House, The Robert E Lee Memorial
description: historic Custis family mansion operated by the U.S. National Park Service, located within Arlington National Cemetery
description_wiki: Arlington House is the historic Custis family mansion built by George Washington Parke Custis from 1803–1818 as a memorial to George Washington.

description: former skyscraper complex in Manhattan, New York (1968-2001)
description_wiki: The original World Trade Center (WTC) was a complex of seven buildings in the Financial District of Lower Manhattan in New York City.

aliases: Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (2025–present)
Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense (1947–2025)
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (1789–1947)
description: headquarters of the United States Department of Defense; named after the shape of the building, a polygon with five (greek: penta) sides
description_wiki: The Pentagon is the headquarters of the United States Department of Defense, in Arlington County, Virginia, across the Potomac River from Washington, D.C. The building was constructed on an accelerated schedule during World War II. As a symbol of the U.S. military, the phrase The Pentagon is often used as a metonym for the Department of Defense and its leadership.

description: unincorporated community in the census-designated place of McLean in Fairfax County, Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Langley is an unincorporated community in the census-designated place of McLean in Fairfax County, Virginia, United States.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

description: unincorporated community located in Northern Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Chantilly is a census-designated place (CDP) in western Fairfax County, Virginia, United States.

description: neighborhood in Arlington, Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Rosslyn ( ROZ-lin) is a heavily urbanized unincorporated area in northeastern Arlington County, Virginia, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Mecklenburg County, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Charlotte is the most populous city in the U.S. state of North Carolina.

description: section of U.S. Numbered Highway in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: U.S. Route 50 (US 50) is a transcontinental highway which stretches from Ocean City, Maryland to West Sacramento, California.

description: city in the state of Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Alexandria is an independent city in the U.S. state of Virginia.
Vienna () is a town in Fairfax County, Virginia, United States, and a suburb of Washington D.C.  As of the 2020 U.S. census, Vienna had a population of 16,473.

description: reserve force of the United States Army and Air Force
description_wiki: The National Guard is a military reserve organization of the United States Department of Defense (DoD).

description: daily broadsheet newspaper in Washington, D.C.
description_wiki: The Washington Post (locally known as The Post and, informally, WaPo or WP) is an American daily newspaper published in Washington, D.C. It is the most widely circulated newspaper in the Washington metropolitan area and is considered a newspaper of record in the United States.

surface_forms: the attack on the World Trade Center
description_wiki: The September 11 attacks, colloquially known as 9/11, were a coordinated series of suicide attacks perpetrated by the Islamist terrorist organization al-Qaeda against the United States in 2001.
